value.found_quote: Dynamic workflows | A script that runs many subagents and cross-checks their results, for work too big to coordinate one turn at a time or that needs more than a single pass | A job outgrows a handful of subagents, or you want findings verified against each other: a codebase-wide audit, a 500-file migration, cross-checked research, or a plan drafted from several angles
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes "Dynamic workflows" as an offering from Anthropic (via Claude Code), characterizing it as "A script that runs many subagents and cross-checks their results, for work too big to coordinate one turn at a time." This closely matches the claim's description of "A script-driven approach that runs many subagents and cross-checks their results for large jobs that exceed what turn-by-turn agentic loops can handle." The page presents it as an active, documented feature (with its own dedicated page at /en/workflows) without any preview, beta, or deprecated markers.
